name: node-electron-to-chromium
title: node-electron-to-chromium
summary: Provides a list of electron-to-chromium version mappings
description:
Provides a list of electron-to-chromium version mappings
This repository provides a mapping of Electron versions to the Chromium
version that it uses.
.
This package is used in Browserslist, so you can use e.g. electron >= 1.4 in
Autoprefixer, Stylelint, babel-preset-env and eslint-plugin-compat.
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use node-electron-to-chromium because Provides a list of electron-to-chromium version mappings.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use node-electron-to-chromium as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair node-electron-to-chromium with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `node-electron-to-chromium --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
